factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, supported by record orders and backlog.

evidence.text: For the quarter ended March 31, 2018, the Company posted record orders, backlog, sales and operating income, as well as strong operating income margins, net income, diluted earnings per share and operating cash flow.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2018 compared with the first quarter of 2017

evidence.text: As a result, the Companys backlog of unfilled orders at March 31, 2018 was $1,568.3 million, an increase of $172.2 million or 12.3%, compared with $1,396.1 million at December 31, 2017.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2018 compared with the first quarter of 2017

evidence.text: The increase in net sales for the first quarter of 2018 was due to 8% organic sales growth, a 5% increase from acquisitions and favorable 3% effect of foreign currency translation.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2018 compared with the first quarter of 2017

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by real demand.

evidence.text: For the quarter ended March 31, 2018, the Company posted record orders, backlog, sales and operating income, as well as strong operating income margins, net income, diluted earnings per share and operating cash flow.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2018 compared with the first quarter of 2017

factors.summary: End markets are growing, particularly in international sales.

evidence.text: Both reportable segments of the Company maintain strong international sales presences in Europe and Asia.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2018 compared with the first quarter of 2017

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding.

evidence.text: Segment operating income, as a percentage of net sales, increased to 23.4% for the first quarter of 2018, compared with 23.2% for the first quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2018 compared with the first quarter of 2017

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: The increase in segment operating income and segment operating margins for the first quarter of 2018 resulted primarily from the increase in net sales noted above, as well as the benefits of the Companys Operational Excellence initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2018 compared with the first quarter of 2017

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to Operational Excellence initiatives.

evidence.text: The increase in segment operating income and segment operating margins for the first quarter of 2018 resulted primarily from the increase in net sales noted above, as well as the benefits of the Companys Operational Excellence initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2018 compared with the first quarter of 2017

factors.summary: Management expects positive impact on 2018 results from market trends and Operational Excellence initiatives.

evidence.text: For 2018, positive market trends, the Companys record backlog, the full year impact of the 2018 and 2017 acquisitions and continued focus on and implementation of Operational Excellence initiatives are expected to have a positive impact on the remainder of the Companys 2018 results.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2018 compared with the first quarter of 2017

factors.summary: Management is confident, evidenced by increased dividends and share buybacks.

evidence.text: Effective February 1, 2018, the Companys Board of Directors approved a 56% increase in the quarterly cash dividend on the Companys common stock to $0.14 per common share from $0.09 per common share.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear with focus on acquisitions and Operational Excellence.

evidence.text: For 2018, positive market trends, the Companys record backlog, the full year impact of the 2018 and 2017 acquisitions and continued focus on and implementation of Operational Excellence initiatives are expected to have a positive impact on the remainder of the Companys 2018 results.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2018 compared with the first quarter of 2017

factors.summary: Debt levels are stable with slight improvement in net debt-to-capital ratio.

evidence.text: The net debt-to-capital ratio was 28.2% at March 31, 2018, compared with 27.5% at December 31, 2017.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing with higher dividends.

evidence.text: Effective February 1, 2018, the Companys Board of Directors approved a 56% increase in the quarterly cash dividend on the Companys common stock to $0.14 per common share from $0.09 per common share.
Free cash flow was $165.1 million for the first three months of 2018, compared with $129.1 million for the first three months of 2017.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are beneficial, reducing interest expense.
